[http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Cqqqq1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Cqqqq1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to cqqqq5]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to cqqqq5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tamanooya Jinja
玉祖神社 | image = Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | caption = Haiden of Tamanooya Jinja Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Tamanooya-no-Mikoto; Ishikori-dome no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =1690 Ōaza Ōsaki, Hōfu-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 747-0065 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Jinja'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Ōsaki neighborhood of the city of [[Hōfu, Yamaguchi|Hōfu]] in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Suō Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tamanooya Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]'''| 玉祖命|}}, the son of [[Ninigi]] and the creator of the ''[[magatama#Yasakani no Magatama|Yasakani no Magatama]]''. * {{nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''| 石凝姥命|}}, the goddess of mirrors and stonecutters, and creator of the ''[[Yata no Kagami]]''. ==History== The origins of Tamanooya Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:qq]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:qq 6]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:Bump-01-21]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Cqqq1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 1]]\[[Category:Cqqq1]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:qq 2]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to cqqq5]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to cqqq5]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]]

Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. {{Infobox religious building | name = Tamanooya Jinja
玉祖神社 | image = Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | caption = Haiden of Tamanooya Jinja Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Tamanooya-no-Mikoto; Ishikori-dome no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =1690 Ōaza Ōsaki, Hōfu-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 747-0065 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Jinja'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Ōsaki neighborhood of the city of [[Hōfu, Yamaguchi|Hōfu]] in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Suō Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tamanooya Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]'''| 玉祖命|}}, the son of [[Ninigi]] and the creator of the ''[[magatama#Yasakani no Magatama|Yasakani no Magatama]]''. * {{nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''| 石凝姥命|}}, the goddess of mirrors and stonecutters, and creator of the ''[[Yata no Kagami]]''. ==History== The origins of Tamanooya Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:11 1]]\[[Category:C111]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:tier 5]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{Ichinomiya}} [[Category:New]]

Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]][[Category:11 6]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:tier 2]]\[[Category:C111]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:if these are dates then move them to c115]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{Ichinomiya}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]][[Category:tier 2]]\[[Category:C111]][[Category:tier 2]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{Ichinomiya}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tamanooya Jinja
玉祖神社 | image = Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | caption = Haiden of Tamanooya Jinja Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Tamanooya-no-Mikoto; Ishikori-dome no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =1690 Ōaza Ōsaki, Hōfu-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 747-0065 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Jinja'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Ōsaki neighborhood of the city of [[Hōfu, Yamaguchi|Hōfu]] in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Suō Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tamanooya Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]'''| 玉祖命|}}, the son of [[Ninigi]] and the creator of the ''[[magatama#Yasakani no Magatama|Yasakani no Magatama]]''. * {{nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''| 石凝姥命|}}, the goddess of mirrors and stonecutters, and creator of the ''[[Yata no Kagami]]''. ==History== The origins of Tamanooya Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]]{{Beppyo Shrines}}[[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]]]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]][[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:11]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]][[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]]\[[Category:C111]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:11]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:11]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] {{Ichinomiya}} [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. {{Infobox religious building | name = Tamanooya Jinja
玉祖神社 | image = Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | caption = Haiden of Tamanooya Jinja Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Tamanooya-no-Mikoto; Ishikori-dome no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =1690 Ōaza Ōsaki, Hōfu-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 747-0065 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Jinja'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Ōsaki neighborhood of the city of [[Hōfu, Yamaguchi|Hōfu]] in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Suō Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tamanooya Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]'''| 玉祖命|}}, the son of [[Ninigi]] and the creator of the ''[[magatama#Yasakani no Magatama|Yasakani no Magatama]]''. * {{nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''| 石凝姥命|}}, the goddess of mirrors and stonecutters, and creator of the ''[[Yata no Kagami]]''. ==History== The origins of Tamanooya Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:11 6]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]]

The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:C111]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tamanooya Jinja
玉祖神社 | image = Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | caption = Haiden of Tamanooya Jinja Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Tamanooya-no-Mikoto; Ishikori-dome no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =1690 Ōaza Ōsaki, Hōfu-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 747-0065 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Jinja'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Ōsaki neighborhood of the city of [[Hōfu, Yamaguchi|Hōfu]] in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Suō Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tamanooya Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]'''| 玉祖命|}}, the son of [[Ninigi]] and the creator of the ''[[magatama#Yasakani no Magatama|Yasakani no Magatama]]''. * {{nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''| 石凝姥命|}}, the goddess of mirrors and stonecutters, and creator of the ''[[Yata no Kagami]]''. ==History== The origins of Tamanooya Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] }} [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]]

The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:bump February 13, 2025 bump]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tamanooya Jinja
玉祖神社 | image = Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | caption = Haiden of Tamanooya Jinja Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Tamanooya-no-Mikoto; Ishikori-dome no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =1690 Ōaza Ōsaki, Hōfu-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 747-0065 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Jinja'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Ōsaki neighborhood of the city of [[Hōfu, Yamaguchi|Hōfu]] in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Suō Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tamanooya Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]'''| 玉祖命|}}, the son of [[Ninigi]] and the creator of the ''[[magatama#Yasakani no Magatama|Yasakani no Magatama]]''. * {{nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''| 石凝姥命|}}, the goddess of mirrors and stonecutters, and creator of the ''[[Yata no Kagami]]''. ==History== The origins of Tamanooya Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]]

The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[ja:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:Ja import failed]]

{{Infobox religious building | name = Tamanooya Jinja
玉祖神社 | image = Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), haiden.jpg | caption = Haiden of Tamanooya Jinja Shrine {{Infobox mapframe|tyep=shape|zoom=14|frame-width=240}} | map_type = Japan Yamaguchi Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = | coordinates = {{coord|34|3|27.75|N|131|32|1.15|E|region:JP-35_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_relief = 1 | map_size = | map_caption = | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | type = | deity = Tamanooya-no-Mikoto; Ishikori-dome no Mikoto | founded_by = | established = unknown | date_destroyed = | location =1690 Ōaza Ōsaki, Hōfu-shi, Yamaguchi-ken 747-0065 | website = | architecture_style = | festival = Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25 | leadership = }} {{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Jinja'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[Shinto shrine]] in the Ōsaki neighborhood of the city of [[Hōfu, Yamaguchi|Hōfu]] in [[Yamaguchi Prefecture]], [[Japan]]. It is the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of former [[Suō Province]]. The main festival of the shrine is held annually on the Saturday & Sunday nearest September 25.{{cite book |last1=Shibuya |first1=Nobuhiro |title=Shokoku jinja Ichinomiya Ninomiya San'nomiya |date=2015 |publisher= Yamakawa shuppansha |isbn=978-4634150867 |language=Japanese}} ==Enshrined ''kami''== The ''[[kami]]'' enshrined at Tamanooya Jinja are: * {{nihongo|'''[[Tamanooya-no-Mikoto]]'''| 玉祖命|}}, the son of [[Ninigi]] and the creator of the ''[[magatama#Yasakani no Magatama|Yasakani no Magatama]]''. * {{nihongo|'''Ishikori-dome no Mikoto'''| 石凝姥命|}}, the goddess of mirrors and stonecutters, and creator of the ''[[Yata no Kagami]]''. ==History== The origins of Tamanooya Jinja are unknown. Although there is no documentary evidence, it is believed that it began as the family shrine for a clan of [[Kofun period]] craftsmen who made ''magatama'' and tubular beads, who regarded Tamanooya-no-Mikoto as their ancestor. In the ''[[Nihon Shoki]]'', when the legendary [[Emperor Keikō]] led an expedition to conquer the [[Kumaso]], he was created by a clan headed by Princess Kamikashi, who offered him sacred treasures, and he prayed at the Tamanooya Jinja for victory. The shrine is mentioned in the "[[Nihon Sandai Jitsuroku]]" and ''[[Engishiki]]'' records from the early [[Heian period]], and was regarded as the ''[[ichinomiya]]'' of the province from this time.{{cite book |last1=Yoshiki |first1=Emi |title=Zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' tettei gaido |date=2007 |publisher=PHP Institute |isbn=978-4569669304 |language=Japanese}} During the Kamakura period, the monk [[Chōgen (monk)|Chōgen]] who visited the Saba River basin to procure materials for the reconstruction of reconstruction of [[Tōdai-ji]] and from March 1186 the province donated substantial portion of its revenues to the project. When the reconstruction was completed in 1195, Chōgen rebuilt the Tamanooya Jinja as a gesture of thanksgiving, and the shrine has preserved the {{nihongo|'''List of Treasures of the Suō Province Ichinomiya Reconstruction'''|周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録| Suō koku ichinomiya Zōtai shinden takaramono-tō mokuroku}} written by Chōgen listing the work accomplished. This document is noteworthy as the most credible example of Chōgen's handwriting and is a National [[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]].{{cite web |url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/9001|title=周防国一宮造替神殿宝物等目録〈建久六年九月廿八日／重源加判〉|trans-title=List of treasures, etc. of the Suō Kuni Ichinomiya Reconstruction Temple|language=Japanese |publisher=[[Agency for Cultural Affairs]] |accessdate=August 20, 2020}} During the [[Meiji period]] era of [[State Shinto]], the shrine was rated as a {{nihongo|Imperial shrine, 2nd Rank |国幣中社|[[kokuhei-chūsha]]}} under the [[Modern system of Ranked Shinto Shrines]]Ponsonby-Fane, Richard. (1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[sn:amanooya Shrine]] [[sn: failed]]

(1959). ''The Imperial House of Japan,'' pp. 125. The shrine is located a 45-minute walk from [[Hōfu Station]] on the [[JR West]] [[Sanyō Main Line]].{{cite book |last1=Okada |first1=Shoji |title=Taiyō no chizuchō 24 zenkoku 'Ichinomiya' meguri |date=2014 |publisher=Heibonsha |isbn=978-4582945614 |language=Japanese}} ==Gallery== File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shaden.jpg|Shaden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), honden.jpg|Honden File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), shinmon.jpg|Gate File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), ichinotorii-1.jpg|Ichi-no-Torii File:Tamanooya-jinja (Hofu), Tamanoiwaya.jpg|Tama-no-iwaya ==See also== *[[List of Shinto shrines]] *[[Ichinomiya]] ==References== * Plutschow, Herbe. ''Matsuri: The Festivals of Japan''. RoutledgeCurzon (1996) {{ISBN|1-873410-63-8}} * [[Richard Ponsonby-Fane|Ponsonby-Fane]], Richard Arthur Brabazon. (1959). [https://books.google.com/books?id=SLAeAAAAMAAJ&q=The+Imperial+House+of+Japan ''The Imperial House of Japan.''] Kyoto: Ponsonby Memorial Society. [http://www.worldcat.org/wcpa/oclc/194887 OCLC 194887] ==External links== {{commons Category|Tamanooya-jinja}} *[https://visit-hofu.jp/spots/tamanooya-shrine Hofu Tourist information] {{Beppyo Shrines}} ==Notes== {{reflist}} {{Shinto shrine}} ]] {{Beppyo Shrines}} \ {{Ichinomiya}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Beppyo shrines]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:beppyo shrines without templates]] [[Category:Shikinai Shosha]] [[Category:Pages that used to use Authority control]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Yamaguchi Prefecture]] [[Category:Suō Province]] [[Category:Hōfu, Yamaguchi]] [[Category:Ichinomiya]] [[Category:Kokuhei Chūsha]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 6]] [[Category:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:pages to be overwritten]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[en:Tamanooya Shrine]] [[sn:amanooya Shrine]] [[sn: failed]]

{{神社 |名称=玉祖神社 |画像=[[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[神立 (八尾市)|神立]]5丁目5−93 | 緯度度 = 34 | 緯度分 = 38 | 緯度秒 = 5.6 | N(北緯)及びS(南緯) = N | 経度度 = 135 |経度分 = 39 | 経度秒 = 9.3 | E(東経)及びW(西経) = E | 地図国コード = JP | 地図 = Japan Osaka |祭神=[[天明玉命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=（伝）[[和銅]]3年（[[710年]]） |本殿=[[春日造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= [[7月]]中頃（夏例祭）、
[[10月10日]]（秋例祭） |神事= }} '''玉祖神社'''（たまのおやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[神立 (八尾市)|神立]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。「玉祖明神」・「高安大明神」とも。 八尾市東部・[[高安 (八尾市)|高安地区]]（恩智地区を除く旧[[高安郡]]13ヶ村）の[[氏神]]である。 ==祭神== *[[天明玉命]]（櫛明玉命） ==歴史== 社伝によると創始は710年（和銅3年）に[[周防国]]の[[玉祖神社]]から分霊を勧請したもので、その際、[[住吉津]]から上陸し、[[恩智神社]]に泊まった後、現在地に祀られたとされている。この地に玉造部の人々が住んでいたため、その祖神を祀ったものと考えられる。現在の社殿は、1725年（享保10年）の再建とされている。 ==境内== [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（八尾市指定文化財）}}]] 参道の石段の脇に大きな[[クスノキ]]が生えている。1949年（昭和24年）に大阪府の[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。同じく参道の石段下の脇には、かつては[[神宮寺]]として「薗光寺竹之坊」があったが、明治維新後の神仏分離により廃寺となった[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33　玉祖神社 - 八尾市文化財情報システム]2019年02月10日　閲覧。 本神社の少し北側を[[大阪市]]の[[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]を起点とする[[俊徳街道・十三街道|十三街道]]が通り、かつては交通の要所となっていた。十三街道によって玉祖と玉造がつながり、両者の地名から何らかの関連があったと考えられている。神社すぐ横で、神立茶屋辻付近から大字服部川の神光寺付近まで南北に通る[[農道]]（八尾農免道）の新設・拡張工事が行われていたが2010年に竣工している。 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|クスノキ（大阪府指定天然記念物） File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|境内鳥居 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|社頭鳥居 File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|一の鳥居 ==祭事== 毎年7月中頃(15日、16日に近い土日)には各地区から布団太鼓が出て祭礼が行われている。2010年（平成22年）には鎮座1300年にあたり、そのための盛大な祭が行われた。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 木造制札 文治元年十二月日トアリ（古文書） *: 玉祖神社の神宮寺に当たる薗光寺（おんこうじ）に掲げられていた制札とされており、1185年(文治元年)12月に[[北条時政]]が出した、日本最古の禁制（きんぜい）とされている[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9　玉祖神社　木造制札（もくぞうせいさつ） - 八尾市文化財情報システム]2019年02月10日　閲覧。八尾市立歴史民俗資料館寄託。1910年（明治43年）4月20日指定明治43年4月20日内務省告示第68号{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|木造制札〈文治元年十二月日トアリ／〉}}。 === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉祖神社 木造 男女神像（彫刻） - かつて神体とされた木造の男女神坐像。1965年（昭和40年）に大阪府重要美術品に指定{{refnest|group=注釈|「大阪府古文化紀念物等保存顕彰規則」に基づき指定されたもので、国から[[重要美術品等ノ保存ニ関スル法律]]により認定される「[[重要美術品]]」とは異なる。}}。1970年（昭和45年）2月20日に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の八尾市ファイルより。。 * 天然記念物 ** 玉祖神社のくす - 1970年（昭和45年）2月20日指定。 === 八尾市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉祖神社本殿（附 棟札）（建造物） - 2016年（平成28年）3月1日指定。 === その他 === 平安時代の歌人の[[在原業平]]が使用していたとされる笛や、[[豊臣秀頼]]寄進の石灯篭などがある。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]神立5丁目5−93（大字神立443番地） ;交通アクセス * 鉄道：[[近鉄信貴線]][[服部川駅]]下車後、徒歩約27分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== *八尾の史跡 （八尾市刊行物番号H10-103） ==関連項目== *[[俊徳街道・十三街道]] *[[玉祖神社]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html 玉祖神社] - 大阪府神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]]

八尾市文化財情報システム]2019年02月10日　閲覧。 本神社の少し北側を[[大阪市]]の[[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]を起点とする[[俊徳街道・十三街道|十三街道]]が通り、かつては交通の要所となっていた。十三街道によって玉祖と玉造がつながり、両者の地名から何らかの関連があったと考えられている。神社すぐ横で、神立茶屋辻付近から大字服部川の神光寺付近まで南北に通る[[農道]]（八尾農免道）の新設・拡張工事が行われていたが2010年に竣工している。 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|クスノキ（大阪府指定天然記念物） File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|境内鳥居 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|社頭鳥居 File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|一の鳥居 ==祭事== 毎年7月中頃(15日、16日に近い土日)には各地区から布団太鼓が出て祭礼が行われている。2010年（平成22年）には鎮座1300年にあたり、そのための盛大な祭が行われた。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 木造制札 文治元年十二月日トアリ（古文書） *: 玉祖神社の神宮寺に当たる薗光寺（おんこうじ）に掲げられていた制札とされており、1185年(文治元年)12月に[[北条時政]]が出した、日本最古の禁制（きんぜい）とされている[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9　玉祖神社　木造制札（もくぞうせいさつ） - 八尾市文化財情報システム]2019年02月10日　閲覧。八尾市立歴史民俗資料館寄託。1910年（明治43年）4月20日指定明治43年4月20日内務省告示第68号{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|木造制札〈文治元年十二月日トアリ／〉}}。 === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉祖神社 木造 男女神像（彫刻） - かつて神体とされた木造の男女神坐像。1965年（昭和40年）に大阪府重要美術品に指定{{refnest|group=注釈|「大阪府古文化紀念物等保存顕彰規則」に基づき指定されたもので、国から[[重要美術品等ノ保存ニ関スル法律]]により認定される「[[重要美術品]]」とは異なる。}}。1970年（昭和45年）2月20日に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の八尾市ファイルより。。 * 天然記念物 ** 玉祖神社のくす - 1970年（昭和45年）2月20日指定。 === 八尾市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉祖神社本殿（附 棟札）（建造物） - 2016年（平成28年）3月1日指定。 === その他 === 平安時代の歌人の[[在原業平]]が使用していたとされる笛や、[[豊臣秀頼]]寄進の石灯篭などがある。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]神立5丁目5−93（大字神立443番地） ;交通アクセス * 鉄道：[[近鉄信貴線]][[服部川駅]]下車後、徒歩約27分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== *八尾の史跡 （八尾市刊行物番号H10-103） ==関連項目== *[[俊徳街道・十三街道]] *[[玉祖神社]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html 玉祖神社] - 大阪府神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]]

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

{{nihongo|'''Tamanooya Shrine'''|玉祖神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Kawachi Province]].

[[JA:玉祖神社 (八尾市)]]

{{wikidata link|Q11572730}}
[[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]]
[[Category:Kanpei-sha]]

{{神社 |名称=玉祖神社 |画像=[[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
拝殿 |所在地=[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[神立 (八尾市)|神立]]5丁目5−93 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神=[[天明玉命]] |社格=[[式内社]]（小）
旧[[郷社]] |創建=（伝）[[和銅]]3年（[[710年]]） |本殿=[[春日造]] |別名= |札所等= |例祭= [[7月]]中頃（夏例祭）、
[[10月10日]]（秋例祭） |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=玉祖神社|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''玉祖神社'''（たまのおやじんじゃ）は、[[大阪府]][[八尾市]][[神立 (八尾市)|神立]]にある[[神社]]。[[式内社]]で、[[近代社格制度|旧社格]]は[[郷社]]。「玉祖明神」・「高安大明神」とも。 八尾市東部・[[高安 (八尾市)|高安地区]]（恩智地区を除く旧[[高安郡]]13ヶ村）の[[氏神]]である。 ==祭神== *[[天明玉命]]（櫛明玉命） ==歴史== 社伝によると創始は710年（和銅3年）に[[周防国]]の[[玉祖神社]]から分霊を勧請したもので、その際、[[住吉津]]から上陸し、[[恩智神社]]に泊まった後、現在地に祀られたとされている。この地に玉造部の人々が住んでいたため、その祖神を祀ったものと考えられる。現在の社殿は、1725年（享保10年）の再建とされている。 ==境内== [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|本殿（八尾市指定文化財）}}]] 参道の石段の脇に大きな[[クスノキ]]が生えている。1949年（昭和24年）に大阪府の[[天然記念物]]に指定されている。同じく参道の石段下の脇には、かつては[[神宮寺]]として「薗光寺竹之坊」があったが、明治維新後の神仏分離により廃寺となった[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33　玉祖神社 - 八尾市文化財情報システム]2019年02月10日　閲覧。 本神社の少し北側を[[大阪市]]の[[玉造 (大阪市)|玉造]]を起点とする[[俊徳街道・十三街道|十三街道]]が通り、かつては交通の要所となっていた。十三街道によって玉祖と玉造がつながり、両者の地名から何らかの関連があったと考えられている。神社すぐ横で、神立茶屋辻付近から大字服部川の神光寺付近まで南北に通る[[農道]]（八尾農免道）の新設・拡張工事が行われていたが2010年に竣工している。 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|クスノキ（大阪府指定天然記念物） File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|境内社 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|境内社 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|境内鳥居 File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|社頭鳥居 File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|一の鳥居 ==祭事== 毎年7月中頃(15日、16日に近い土日)には各地区から布団太鼓が出て祭礼が行われている。2010年（平成22年）には鎮座1300年にあたり、そのための盛大な祭が行われた。 == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 木造制札 文治元年十二月日トアリ（古文書） *: 玉祖神社の神宮寺に当たる薗光寺（おんこうじ）に掲げられていた制札とされており、1185年(文治元年)12月に[[北条時政]]が出した、日本最古の禁制（きんぜい）とされている[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9　玉祖神社　木造制札（もくぞうせいさつ） - 八尾市文化財情報システム]2019年02月10日　閲覧。八尾市立歴史民俗資料館寄託。1910年（明治43年）4月20日指定明治43年4月20日内務省告示第68号{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|木造制札〈文治元年十二月日トアリ／〉}}。 === 大阪府指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉祖神社 木造 男女神像（彫刻） - かつて神体とされた木造の男女神坐像。1965年（昭和40年）に大阪府重要美術品に指定{{refnest|group=注釈|「大阪府古文化紀念物等保存顕彰規則」に基づき指定されたもので、国から[[重要美術品等ノ保存ニ関スル法律]]により認定される「[[重要美術品]]」とは異なる。}}。1970年（昭和45年）2月20日に大阪府指定有形文化財に指定[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html 大阪府内指定文化財一覧表]（大阪府ホームページ）の八尾市ファイルより。。 * 天然記念物 ** 玉祖神社のくす - 1970年（昭和45年）2月20日指定。 === 八尾市指定文化財 === * 有形文化財 ** 玉祖神社本殿（附 棟札）（建造物） - 2016年（平成28年）3月1日指定。 === その他 === 平安時代の歌人の[[在原業平]]が使用していたとされる笛や、[[豊臣秀頼]]寄進の石灯篭などがある。 == 現地情報 == ;所在地 *[[大阪府]][[八尾市]]神立5丁目5−93（大字神立443番地） ;交通アクセス * 鉄道：[[近鉄信貴線]][[服部川駅]]下車後、徒歩約27分 == 脚注 == === 注釈 === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === 出典 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} ==参考文献== *八尾の史跡 （八尾市刊行物番号H10-103） ==関連項目== *[[俊徳街道・十三街道]] *[[玉祖神社]] ==外部リンク== {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html 玉祖神社] - 大阪府神社庁 {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]]
