[[JA:玉祖神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11572730}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tamanooya Shrine |画像=[[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}{{jalink|Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|Kozaki}} 5-5-93 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{jalink|Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto}} |社格={{jalink|Shikinaisha}} (small)
former {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}} |創建=(trad.) {{jalink|Wado|Q1373026}} 3 ({{jalink|710|Q23518}}) |本殿={{jalink|kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Mid-{{jalink|July|Q121}} (summer annual festival),
{{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|shrine|Q845945}} in {{jalink|Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|Kozaki}}, {{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}, {{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}}, and its former rank under the {{jalink|modern shrine ranking system|Q712534}} was {{jalink|Gosha|Q134917316}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{jalink|ujigami|Q767655}} of the eastern Yao {{jalink|Takayasu area|Q11669131|Takayasu district}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{jalink|Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} in {{jalink|Suo Province|Q1053037}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{jalink|Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628}}, stayed at {{jalink|Onji Shrine|Q11492258}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{jalink|camphor tree|Q158722}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{jalink|natural monument|Q23790}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{jalink|jinguuji|Q1074971}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{jalink|Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{jalink|Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|Tamatsukuri}} in {{jalink|Osaka|Q35765}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{jalink|farm road|Q11638045}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{jalink|Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{jalink|Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{jalink|Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991}} and a stone lantern donated by {{jalink|Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{jalink|Osaka Prefecture|Q122723}}{{jalink|Yao|Q490872}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208}} on the {{jalink|Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{jalink|Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622}} *{{jalink|Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill||lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. [[JA:玉祖神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11572730}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tamanooya Shrine |画像=[[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}{{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki}} 5-5-93 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3 ({{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Mid-{{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} (summer annual festival),
{{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{commons category|Category:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} [[JA:玉祖神社 (八尾市)]] {{wikidata link|Q11572730}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称=Tamanooya Shrine |画像=[[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}{{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki}} 5-5-93 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026}} 3 ({{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Mid-{{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121}} (summer annual festival),
{{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

|画像=[[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), haiden-2.jpg|280px]]
Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki|1=Kozaki}} 5-5-93 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518|1=710}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Mid-{{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}} (summer annual festival),
{{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki|1=Kozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district|1=Takayasu}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628|1=Suminoe no Tsu}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido|1=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri|1=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045|1=Agricultural road}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864|1=Law Regarding the Preservation of Important Works of Fine Arts}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991|1=Ariwara no Narihira}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|1=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido|1=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri|1=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045|1=Agricultural road}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864|1=Law Regarding the Preservation of Important Works of Fine Arts}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991|1=Ariwara no Narihira}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|1=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki|1=Kozaki}} 5-5-93 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518|1=710}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Mid-{{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}} (summer annual festival),
{{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki|1=Kozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district|1=Takayasu}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628|1=Suminoe no Tsu}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido|1=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri|1=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045|1=Agricultural road}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864|1=Law Regarding the Preservation of Important Works of Fine Arts}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991|1=Ariwara no Narihira}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|1=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} {{DEFAULTSORT:たまのおやしんしや やおし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]]

Haiden |所在地={{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}{{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki|1=Kozaki}} 5-5-93 |位置= {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格={{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} (small)
former {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建=(trad.) {{ill|lt=Wado|Q1373026|WD=Q1373026|1=Wadō (era)}} 3 ({{ill|lt=710|Q23518|WD=Q23518|1=710}}) |本殿={{ill|lt=kasuga-zukuri|Q3193781|WD=Q3193781|1=Kasuga-zukuri}} |別名= |札所等= |例祭= Mid-{{ill|lt=July|Q121|WD=Q121|1=July}} (summer annual festival),
{{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki|1=Kozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district|1=Takayasu}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628|1=Suminoe no Tsu}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido|1=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri|1=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045|1=Agricultural road}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864|1=Law Regarding the Preservation of Important Works of Fine Arts}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991|1=Ariwara no Narihira}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|1=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]][[Category:qq]]

{{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} (autumn annual festival) |神事= |地図= {{Location map|Japan Osaka|label=Tamanooya Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Tamanooya Shrine''' (たまのおやじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kozaki (Yao)|Q11590720|WD=Q11590720|lt=Kozaki|1=Kozaki}}, {{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}, {{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}, and its former rank under the {{ill|lt=modern shrine ranking system|Q712534|WD=Q712534|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines}} was {{ill|lt=Gosha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. It is also called "Tamanooya Myojin" and "Takayasu Daimyojin." It is the {{ill|lt=ujigami|Q767655|WD=Q767655|1=Ujigami}} of the eastern Yao {{ill|lt=Takayasu area|Q11669131|WD=Q11669131|lt=Takayasu district|1=Takayasu}} (13 former villages of old Takayasu District excluding the Onji area). === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Ame-no-Akama-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (also called Kushi-Akama-no-Mikoto) === History === According to shrine tradition, it began in 710 (Wado 3) when a divided spirit was invited from {{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} in {{ill|lt=Suo Province|Q1053037|WD=Q1053037|1=Suō Province}}. At that time the deity is said to have landed at {{ill|lt=Suminoe-tsu|Q3503628|WD=Q3503628|1=Suminoe no Tsu}}, stayed at {{ill|lt=Onji Shrine|Q11492258|WD=Q11492258|1=Onji Shrine}}, and then been enshrined at the present site. Because people of the Tamatsukuri-be lived in this area, it is thought that their ancestral deity was enshrined here. The current shrine buildings are said to have been rebuilt in 1725 (Kyoho 10). === Precincts === [[File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), honden.jpg|thumb|220px|right|{{center|Main hall (Yao City designated cultural property)}}]] A large {{ill|lt=camphor tree|Q158722|WD=Q158722|1=Camphora officinarum}} grows beside the stone steps of the approach. It was designated a {{ill|lt=natural monument|Q23790|WD=Q23790|1=Natural monument}} of Osaka Prefecture in 1949 (Showa 24). Also beside the lower approach steps once stood "Onkoji Takenobo" as the shrine's former {{ill|lt=jinguuji|Q1074971|WD=Q1074971|1=Jingū-ji}} temple, but it was abolished after the Meiji Restoration due to separation of Shinto and Buddhism[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=33 Tamanooya Shrine - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Just north of this shrine runs the {{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|lt=Jusan Kaido|1=Jusan Kaido}}, starting at {{ill|lt=Tamatsukuri (Osaka)|Q11572803|WD=Q11572803|lt=Tamatsukuri|1=Tamatsukuri}} in {{ill|lt=Osaka|Q35765|WD=Q35765|1=Osaka}}, and it was formerly a key transport route. Since Tamanooya and Tamatsukuri are linked by this road, some relationship between the place names is inferred. New and widened construction of a {{ill|lt=farm road|Q11638045|WD=Q11638045|1=Agricultural road}} (Yao Agricultural Exemption Road) running north-south from near Kozaki Chaya-tsuji to near Shinko-ji in Oaza Hattorigawa beside the shrine was completed in 2010. File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), kusu.jpg|Camphor tree (Osaka Prefecture designated natural monument) File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-1.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), keidaisha-2.jpg|Precinct shrine File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-3.jpg|Torii in precincts File:Tamanooya-jinja (Yao), torii-2.jpg|Front torii File:Tamaoya-zinja07.JPG|First torii === Festivals === Every year in mid-July (weekend nearest the 15th and 16th), each district brings out futon-daiko drums for the festival. In 2010 (Heisei 22), the shrine marked its 1300th year and held a grand commemorative festival. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Property (national) === * Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji 1, 12th month" (old document) *: Said to be the prohibition placard displayed at Onkoji, the shrine-temple of Tamanooya Shrine, and regarded as Japan's oldest surviving kinsai (prohibition order), issued by {{ill|lt=Hojo Tokimasa|Q589811|WD=Q589811|1=Hōjō Tokimasa}} in December 1185 (Bunji 1)[http://bunka.city.yao.osaka.jp/detail/index?cultural_id=9 Tamanooya Shrine Wooden Prohibition Placard - Yao City Cultural Property Information System], accessed 2019-02-10. Deposited at Yao City Museum of History and Folklore. Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864|1=Law Regarding the Preservation of Important Works of Fine Arts}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991|1=Ariwara no Narihira}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|1=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:八尾市の建築物]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]]

Designated April 20, 1910 (Meiji 43)Home Ministry Notification No. 68, April 20, Meiji 43{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|9546|Wooden prohibition placard with inscription "Bunji first year twelfth month"}}. === Osaka Prefecture designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural properties ** Tamanooya Shrine wooden male and female deity statues (sculpture) - seated wooden statues once treated as shintai. Designated as Osaka Prefecture important art objects in 1965 (Showa 40){{refnest|group=注釈|This designation is under the Osaka Prefecture rule for preserving and honoring old cultural memorials, and differs from national "Important Art Objects" recognized under the {{ill|lt=Law for the Preservation of Important Art Objects|Q11644864|WD=Q11644864|1=Law Regarding the Preservation of Important Works of Fine Arts}}.}}. Designated as Osaka Prefecture tangible cultural property on February 20, 1970 (Showa 45)[https://www.pref.osaka.lg.jp/bunkazaihogo/bunkazai/hunai-siteiichiran.html List of designated cultural properties in Osaka Prefecture] (Yao City file).. * Natural monument ** Camphor tree of Tamanooya Shrine - designated February 20, 1970 (Showa 45). === Yao City designated cultural properties === * Tangible cultural property ** Tamanooya Shrine main hall (with ridge tags) (building) - designated March 1, 2016 (Heisei 28). === Other === The shrine preserves items such as a flute said to have been used by the Heian poet {{ill|lt=Ariwara no Narihira|Q451991|WD=Q451991|1=Ariwara no Narihira}} and a stone lantern donated by {{ill|lt=Toyotomi Hideyori|Q313641|WD=Q313641|1=Toyotomi Hideyori}}. === Site information === ;Location *{{ill|lt=Osaka Prefecture|Q122723|WD=Q122723|1=Osaka Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yao|Q490872|WD=Q490872|1=Yao, Osaka}}Kozaki 5-5-93 (Oaza Kozaki lot 443) ;Access * Rail: About 27 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Hattorigawa Station|Q5682208|WD=Q5682208|1=Hattorigawa Station}} on the {{ill|lt=Kintetsu Shigi Line|Q874150|WD=Q874150|1=Shigi Line}} === Footnotes === === Notes === {{Reflist|group="注釈"}} === References === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === Bibliography === *Historic Sites of Yao (Yao City publication no. H10-103) === See also === *{{ill|lt=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido|Q11385622|WD=Q11385622|1=Shuntoku-kaido / Jusan-kaido}} *{{ill|lt=Tamanooya Shrine|Q11572731|WD=Q11572731|1=Tamanooya Shrine (Q11572731)}} === External links === {{Commonscat|Tamanooya-jinja (Yao)}} * [http://www.jinjacho-osaka.net/osakafunai-no-jinjya/dai6sibu/yao-city/m13_07_yao_tanooya.html Tamanooya Shrine] - Osaka Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines {{shinto-stub}} {{神道 横}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Tamanooya Shrine]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Shikinai Hiteisha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Kawachi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:大阪府の神社]] [[Category:Buildings in Yao, Osaka]] [[Category:八尾市の歴史]] [[Category:河内国の式内社]] [[Category:大阪府の旧郷社]] [[Category:大阪府の重要文化財]] [[Category:高安 (八尾市)]]
